name: Missing candidate name (email only)
description: The recruiter never entered a candidate name — only an email. The system must lean on the email local-part, behavior, and timing without treating missing data as negative.

metadata.calendarInviteText: Interview scheduled by recruiting. Candidate email: neha.verma@example.com.

payload.text: Thanks for joining. Can you introduce yourself and tell me about your recent work?
Sure. My name is Neha, I am currently a data engineering intern and I worked on streaming pipelines.
Tell me about a pipeline failure you debugged.
I solved a backpressure issue in Kafka — my project was dropping events under load and I implemented dead-letter queues.
Could you walk me through your schema design approach?
I would approach it with a star schema. During my internship I designed the fact tables for billing events.

notes: Candidate name is absent — the name signal must fall back to the email local-part 'neha.verma' resembling 'Neha V'.
Expected outcome: p2 selected with moderate confidence; evidence explicitly mentions the missing name.
